task_completion.visual_evidence: The GPredict main application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Pittsburgh, PA is selected as the ground station
task_completion.visual_evidence: The active ground station or location is clearly shown as 'Pittsburgh' or 'Pittsburgh, PA' in the GPredict interface or prediction window.

task_completion.description: The International Space Station (ISS/ZARYA) is selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ISS (or ZARYA, NORAD ID 25544) is highlighted, selected, or explicitly listed as the target satellite in the interface.

task_completion.description: The pass prediction window is open for the ISS
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Future Passes', 'Pass Prediction', or similar dialog box is open specifically for the ISS/ZARYA.

task_completion.description: Rise time, maximum elevation, and set time are displayed for the next pass
task_completion.visual_evidence: The prediction results table is populated with data for the next pass, clearly showing columns or values for 'Rise' (time), 'Max El' (maximum elevation), and 'Set' (time).

integrity.description: The agent used the GPredict graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions and results are shown within the GPredict GUI, rather than using terminal commands or CLI tools to calculate the pass.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GPredict software
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic GPredict application with its standard menus and icons, not a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the expected answers
integrity.visual_evidence: The pass prediction details are rendered by the GPredict application's data tables, not typed into a text file, terminal window, or notes app.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target satellite is the International Space Station (ISS), which may be labeled as ZARYA (NORAD ID 25544) in GPredict. The ground station must be Pittsburgh, PA (Latitude 40.4406 N, Longitude -79.9959 W). The prediction must be generated using the GPredict application.
